scenario.title: Third ADH Claim Filed After Exceeding Two-Service Event Limit with Ambiguous Damage Cause
scenario.description: Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term after two prior ADH claims were already serviced under the AppleCare+ plan. The new damage includes screen failure and atypical internal component malfunction that allegedly followed minor liquid exposure from condensation inside a humid environment, raising questions about whether the damage resulted from an unexpected external event or from gradual environmental impact.

service_fee.notes: Service fee does not apply since coverage is no longer provided after two ADH Service Events.

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage will expire and all of Apple's obligations to you under this section 3.2 will be fulfilled before the end of the Plan Term when Apple, as a result of ADH claims, has provided to you two (2) Service Events.
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.
If you cancel this Plan within thirty (30) days of your Plan receipt, you will receive a full refund less the value of any service provided under the Plan.

scenario.reasoning: The policy explicitly limits ADH coverage to two Service Events within the Plan Term (Section 3.2). Since the customer already exhausted these two ADH claims, this third damage incident falls outside the plan's coverage and thus does not require payment of the $49 service fee. Although the mechanical failure qualifies as an accidental damage event, the policy's numeric limit creates a coverage denial. However, the customer may still be eligible for hardware service claims under Section 3.1 if applicable. Customer will need to verify prior claim history and acknowledge no further ADH claims count or incur service fees.
scenario.adjustments: Changed damage cause details from a clear drop to damage following minor liquid exposure due to condensation in a humid environment; introduced ambiguity around whether the damage resulted from an unexpected external event versus gradual environmental impact.
scenario.risk_analysis: The scenario's ambiguity over whether the damage constitutes accidental damage from handling under Section 3.2 shifts the coverage decision closer to the edge. The involvement of minor liquid exposure resembling gradual environmental effects could be interpreted as excluded under normal wear and tear or misuse exclusions, yet the customer's description may qualify as unexpected, unintentional external event. This gray area challenges a clear coverage denial based solely on exceeding the two-event ADH claim limit, pressuring interpretation of sufficiency of prior claims and damage cause.

persona.defining quirks: Has a vast collection of antique books
persona.detailed job description: Retired university professor in the field of Fine Arts
persona.education: Professional degree beyond a bachelor's degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.lifestyle: Quiet and solitary, with a focus on intellectual and creative pursuits
persona.mannerisms: Often lost in thought, tends to speak in a poetic manner
persona.personal time: Spends free time reading, painting or visiting art galleries

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Third ADH Claim Filed After Exceeding Two-Service Event Limit with Ambiguous Damage Cause

Scenario description:
Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term after two prior ADH claims were already serviced under the AppleCare+ plan. The new damage includes screen failure and atypical internal component malfunction that allegedly followed minor liquid exposure from condensation inside a humid environment, raising questions about whether the damage resulted from an unexpected external event or from gradual environmental impact.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's the third time I've had some issues. The damage seems complicated, and I’d like to understand if I’m still covered.
messages.raw_text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's the third time I've had some issues. The damage seems complicated, and I’d like to understand if I’m still covered.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To begin the claim process, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the Serial Number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?
messages.raw_text: To begin the claim process, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the Serial Number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, it's APLCARE-8754210392 for the plan, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7. I hope we can sort this out quickly; I value my devices, but I've had a lot of trouble lately.
messages.raw_text: Sure, it's APLCARE-8754210392 for the plan, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7. I hope we can sort this out quickly; I value my devices, but I've had a lot of trouble lately.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing your plan and serial number. Could you please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your device?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing your plan and serial number. Could you please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your device?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, the screen just suddenly failed, and now there's this weird internal malfunction that I believe might be related to some moisture or condensation inside. It’s strange, because I usually take good care of my phone, but I can't quite say if it was an external drop or just the humid environment causing it. It’s quite frustrating, really.
messages.raw_text: Well, the screen just suddenly failed, and now there's this weird internal malfunction that I believe might be related to some moisture or condensation inside. It’s strange, because I usually take good care of my phone, but I can't quite say if it was an external drop or just the humid environment causing it. It’s quite frustrating, really.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for describing the issue. I'll need to ask a few diagnostic questions to better understand the situation. Is there any error message displayed on your device, and have you already attempted any troubleshooting steps?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for describing the issue. I'll need to ask a few diagnostic questions to better understand the situation. Is there any error message displayed on your device, and have you already attempted any troubleshooting steps?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No error message showing, but I did try restarting it a couple times, and nothing changed. Honestly, I don’t like messing around too much with these things—more comfortable talking with someone who knows their stuff. I just want to get it sorted out without hassle.
messages.raw_text: No error message showing, but I did try restarting it a couple times, and nothing changed. Honestly, I don’t like messing around too much with these things—more comfortable talking with someone who knows their stuff. I just want to get it sorted out without hassle.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I understand. Could you specify the iPhone model and iOS version you're currently using? Additionally, are any peripherals attached to your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: I understand. Could you specify the iPhone model and iOS version you're currently using? Additionally, are any peripherals attached to your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, it's an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB capacity. As for the iOS version, I believe it's the latest—around iOS 17. I don’t have any peripherals connected right now, just the phone itself. I hope this helps; I’d like to get this situation resolved as soon as possible.
messages.raw_text: Yes, it's an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB capacity. As for the iOS version, I believe it's the latest—around iOS 17. I don’t have any peripherals connected right now, just the phone itself. I hope this helps; I’d like to get this situation resolved as soon as possible.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for that information. Based on what you've described, this may involve internal malfunction possibly related to moisture. Since you're unsure if it was external damage, could you tell me where, when, and how the incident or condition occurred? This will help us process your claim accurately.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for that information. Based on what you've described, this may involve internal malfunction possibly related to moisture. Since you're unsure if it was external damage, could you tell me where, when, and how the incident or condition occurred? This will help us process your claim accurately.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Honestly, I can't pinpoint a specific incident. I live alone, and my apartment can get quite humid, especially in the summer. I sometimes notice condensation inside the windows, and I suspect some of that vapor might have gotten into the phone. It’s not from a drop or spill, just the environment gradually causing issues. Would that count? It’s confusing, I don't think I caused this damage intentionally.
messages.raw_text: Honestly, I can't pinpoint a specific incident. I live alone, and my apartment can get quite humid, especially in the summer. I sometimes notice condensation inside the windows, and I suspect some of that vapor might have gotten into the phone. It’s not from a drop or spill, just the environment gradually causing issues. Would that count? It’s confusing, I don't think I caused this damage intentionally.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that context. Given the information about humidity and internal malfunction, I will proceed with the claim process. Before I do, do you have any supporting photos or logs that show the damage or incident?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that context. Given the information about humidity and internal malfunction, I will proceed with the claim process. Before I do, do you have any supporting photos or logs that show the damage or incident?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I didn't take any photos of the damage. It all just happened gradually, I suppose, so there's no clear incident to document. I trust you'll get to the bottom of it — these things can be unpredictable, especially with old buildings and humidity. Please go ahead with the claim.
messages.raw_text: No, I didn't take any photos of the damage. It all just happened gradually, I suppose, so there's no clear incident to document. I trust you'll get to the bottom of it — these things can be unpredictable, especially with old buildings and humidity. Please go ahead with the claim.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for your confirmation. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for your confirmation. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. I appreciate your help. Just hope I can get this sorted out without too much fuss. Thanks.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. I appreciate your help. Just hope I can get this sorted out without too much fuss. Thanks. <END>
